title: blepharis procumbens
intro: Blepharis procumbens, also known as the creeping blepharis or creeping forked blue eyes, is a succulent plant native to tropical Africa, characterized by its small, blue, forked flowers and sprawling habit. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of blepharis procumbens include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Blepharis procumbens can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional African medicine to make poultices and salves for topical application.
